record.field: A self-hosted notes app — example template backed by PostgreSQL.

services: app: image: example/notes:1.0 restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - db environment: # A secret stays a ${reference}; its value lands in the runtime .env, never here. - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://notes:${db_password}@db:5432/notes # ${main_domain} resolves to the instance's public domain at deploy. - PUBLIC_URL=https://${main_domain} - SESSION_SECRET=${session_secret} ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' volumes: - ./app_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: unless-stopped user: postgres environment: POSTGRES_USER: notes POSTGRES_DB: notes POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${db_password} volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U notes"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Consistent dump of the running PostgreSQL into $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verify the restore is non-empty (the hard proof that data survived). N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "restored tables: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
